rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 24, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant The Procter & Gamble Manufacturing Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 4 and 6 (negligence), granted as to issues 2, 5 and 6 (products liability and civil battery) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 7 (false representation and punitive damages). As to the negligence claims, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Betty Utterback was exposed to asbestos-products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s separate statement failed to include any purported undisputed facts pertaining to anything other than whether defendant manufactured, sold or supplied products. It did not address Russell Utterback¿s deposition testimony about the work performed by defendant¿s employees in his presence. As to the strict liability claims, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether Betty Utterback was exposed to asbestos-containing products manufactured, sold or supplied by defendant. Plaintiffs represent that they are withdrawing their claims for false representation and punitive damages, although no dismissal is on file yet. Plaintiffs¿ claim for civil liability fails to state sufficient facts to entitle them to relief based on civil battery. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND CRONIN et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 24, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Honeywell International Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and products liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 3, 4 and 5 (false representation, concert of action and punitive damages). Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Cronin was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to submit plaintiffs¿ responses to specillay-prepared contention discovery, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs state that they are dismissing their claims for false representation, concert of action and punitive damages. If the tentative ruling is confirmed, at the conclusion of the hearing on the motion for summary judgment the court will hear argument on defendant¿s ex parte application to continue the trial date which was deferred from March 16. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: NARDINE ALVARADO et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 24, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Schneider Electric USA¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. The declarations and deposition testimony of Mr. Kelly and Dr. Bruch create a triable issue whether Mr. Alvarado was exposed to asbestos-containing Square D arc shields and whether such exposure was a substantial factor increasing his risk of developing an asbestos-related disease. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL LANCASTER II et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 24, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 8. Defendant Exxon Mobil Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Paul Lancaster was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiffs¿ interrogatory responses stated that Mr. Hunter will testify that Mr. Lancaster was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s separate statement failed to demonstrate that Mr. Hunter was unable to provide admissible testify about those facts. (See purported undisputed material facts 4, 8, and 15-19). = (220/HEK)
